detail: Determinate sentencing for offenses on or after December 31, 1999, replacing parole with extended supervision.

event: Geriatric and terminal condition release created
detail: Created petitions to modify a bifurcated sentence at age 65 with 5 years served, age 60 with 10, or with a terminal condition; the program review committee refers and the sentencing court decides.
Replaced the terminal condition ground with the broader extraordinary health condition and moved petition decisions from the sentencing court to the Earned Release Review Commission.
Repealed most early release mechanisms from Act 28, renamed the review commission the Parole Commission, and recreated sec. 302.113(9g) with the sentencing court again deciding petitions.
Implemented extraordinary health condition and geriatric petitions, formalizing eligibility, documentation, and review.
Petition and order forms standardized statewide practice, restating the statutory age and time-served thresholds.
source: Wisconsin Court System forms CR-254, CR-255, CR-256
source_detail: thresholds of 65 and 5 years, or 60 and 10 years

detail: Updated internal processes, documentation standards, committee roles, exclusions, and referral procedures.
Gave victims the right to use visual aids when making statements at sentence modification hearings, without altering eligibility criteria.
